title: Scale-Safe Sprint History + Self-Improvement Memory
description: Hardens agent-bober's append-only event log against unbounded growth, then adds a deterministic, bounded lessons memory the planner reads at plan time to learn from past sprint outcomes. Layer 1 makes history reads scale via crash-safe rotation plus a new bounded reader while preserving loadHistory's full-read contract so resume-cursor and the conformance harness stay byte-equivalent. Layer 2 closes the feedback arc from evaluator outcomes back into planning without ever feeding raw history to a model: distillation is LLM-free and runs only via an explicit CLI command.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Deterministic-only. Distillation aggregates structured fields already present in HistoryEntry.details, EvalResult, and FeedbackResult into LessonEntry records with no LLM or network call. This gives zero reward-hacking surface and full auditability, matching the deterministic/inspectable constraint from the RSI research that motivated the feature.

Explicit CLI command only. Distillation runs via `bober memory distill` and is never invoked from the pipeline or any autopilot path, keeping it outside the in-context-reward-hacking loop. Auto-triggering is deferred behind a future flag.
Both layers as buildable sprint contracts in one PlanSpec, Layer 1 ordered first so the storage hardening ships and de-risks independently before Layer 2 builds on it.

assumptions: loadHistory keeps its full-read contract unchanged; a new loadRecentHistory provides bounded tail reads. (conformance.ts:102 deep-compares the full history; resume-cursor.ts:19 discards loadHistory's return value.)

Rotation keeps the active history.jsonl bounded by a history.maxActiveLines config and moves overflow to history.archive.jsonl; loadHistory reads archive+active concatenated so resume-cursor and conformance observe an unchanged full stream. (conformance.ts:34-51,131-137.)
Bounding history reads cannot change the reconstructed ResumeCursor because resume-cursor.ts derives it from durable contract status and discards loadHistory's result. (resume-cursor.ts:13-32.)
Lessons live in a new .bober/memory/ directory (none exists today), mirroring the MEMORY.md pattern: a bounded INDEX.md plus one markdown file per lesson.

Distillation aggregates structured fields already in HistoryEntry.details, EvalResult, and FeedbackResult and makes no LLM or network call. (history.ts:37-43; evaluator-agent.ts:187 appends eval outcomes to history.)
Distillation runs only via the explicit `bober memory distill` command and is never invoked from pipeline.ts or any autopilot path.
Planner retrieval is deterministic tag/keyword overlap over the bounded index, hard-capped at topK; no embeddings in this plan.
The planner reads the bounded lessons index, never history.jsonl; bober.plan Step 2 and agents/bober-planner.md gain a memory-index read step.
Every LessonEntry carries a non-empty sourceEntryRefs provenance array and is inspectable via `bober memory list`/`show`.

The project is TypeScript with zod schemas and Vitest; new code follows src/state/history.ts and src/cli/commands/sprint.ts conventions. (bober.config.json evaluator strategies require typecheck and build.)
outOfScope: LLM-summarized or LLM-ranked lessons (deferred — deterministic only).
Embeddings, vector, or SQLite-FTS retrieval (deferred until the markdown index outgrows itself).
Auto-triggered distillation inside the pipeline or after each sprint (explicit CLI only this plan).
Self-modification of skill prompts, sprint contracts, or evaluator criteria (separate, gated, future feature).
STOP-style auto-tuning of retries, budgets, agent counts, or model routing.
Feeding memory into the generator or evaluator at sprint time (this plan wires memory into the planner only).
Changing the exported signature of loadHistory or breaking the conformance equivalence contract.
Migrating the existing 272-line history.jsonl (rotation applies to new appends going forward).

features.title: Bounded history reads + crash-safe rotation
features.description: Add a bounded loadRecentHistory reader and crash-safe rotation that moves old entries to history.archive.jsonl once the active log exceeds a configured line cap, while keeping loadHistory's full-read contract intact so resume-cursor and the conformance harness stay byte-equivalent.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When the active history.jsonl exceeds history.maxActiveLines, the oldest entries are moved to history.archive.jsonl and the active file retains at most maxActiveLines entries.
AC2: When loadHistory is called after a rotation, it returns the full ordered entry set identical to the pre-rotation sequence by reading archive then active.
AC3: When loadRecentHistory is called with a limit, it returns at most that many newest entries and reads only the active file, not the archive.
AC4: When a rotation is interrupted between archiving and truncating, the union of active+archive still contains every original entry exactly once (no loss, no duplication).

features.title: Deterministic lessons memory store
features.description: A new append-only .bober/memory/ store: a zod LessonEntry schema with a mandatory provenance array, one markdown file per lesson, and a bounded INDEX.md, plus a capped index reader. Mirrors the MEMORY.md curated-index pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When a lesson is appended, a .bober/memory/<lessonId>.md file is written and exactly one INDEX.md line is upserted for that lessonId.
AC2: When a LessonEntry is validated with an empty sourceEntryRefs array, schema validation rejects it (provenance is mandatory).
AC3: When loadLessonIndex is called with a limit, it returns at most that many records parsed from INDEX.md without opening any individual lesson file.

features.title: Deterministic distillation + bober memory CLI
features.description: A pure, deterministic distill function that compresses history + eval outcomes into provenance-tagged lessons with no LLM or network call, plus a `bober memory distill|list|show` command. Idempotent: re-running adds no duplicate lessons.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When distill runs over a fixed history fixture, it returns the same deterministic LessonEntry set every time, derived only from structured fields.
AC2: When `bober memory distill` is run twice over identical input, the second run adds zero new INDEX.md lines (idempotent dedupe by content hash).
AC3: When distill executes, it constructs no LLM provider client and performs no network call.
AC4: When `bober memory list` and `bober memory show <lessonId>` run, they print the bounded index and a single lesson with its sourceEntryRefs.

features.title: Planner reads bounded memory (close the arc)
features.description: Deterministic retrieveRelevantLessons (tag/keyword overlap, topK-capped, index-only) wired into bober.plan Step 2 and agents/bober-planner.md, so the planner learns from past outcomes without ever reading raw history.jsonl. Ships the demonstrably-improves measure.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When retrieveRelevantLessons is called with feature keywords and a topK, it returns at most topK lessons ranked by deterministic tag/keyword overlap, reading only INDEX.md.
AC2: When the planner gathers context, it loads the bounded lessons index and never issues a read against history.jsonl.
AC3: When more than topK lessons exist, the serialized lessons block passed to the planner is capped at topK entries and a configured character budget.
AC4: The bober.plan skill and bober-planner agent reference the memory index and the topK cap, and the close-the-arc flow plus its findPrecisionIssues A/B measure are documented.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: loadRecentHistory reads at most the active history.jsonl (kept bounded by maxActiveLines via rotation) and never parses the archive.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test seeds entries spanning a rotation and asserts loadRecentHistory returns at most `limit` entries and issues no read against history.archive.jsonl.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Rotation is crash-safe: it neither drops nor duplicates an entry, even when interrupted mid-rotation.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test counts entries before and after, and simulates an interrupted rotation, asserting the active+archive union equals the original set exactly once.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Distillation is deterministic and idempotent: identical input yields an identical lesson index and re-running adds no duplicates.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test runs distill twice over a fixture and asserts an identical INDEX.md with zero added lines on the second run.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The distillation path performs no LLM or network call, and planner retrieval returns at most topK lessons within a bounded character budget.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test asserts no provider client is constructed in the distill path and that retrieveRelevantLessons truncates to topK and the character budget.

techNotes.dataModel: Existing HistoryEntry (src/state/history.ts) split at a line threshold into active history.jsonl + history.archive.jsonl. New LessonEntry { lessonId, createdAt, category, tags: string[], summary, occurrences: positive int, severity: enum, sourceEntryRefs: non-empty string[] } persisted as .bober/memory/<lessonId>.md (front-matter + body) with a bounded .bober/memory/INDEX.md (one line per lesson). No external entities.
securityConsiderations: Distillation is LLM-free and offline: no provider client, no network egress.
Every LessonEntry carries a non-empty sourceEntryRefs provenance array for human audit.
Planner-facing retrieval is hard-capped at topK and a character budget to bound context.
techNotes.existingPatterns: Mirror src/state/history.ts module style (path helpers, zod schema + safeParse, append-only file ops). Add the CLI under src/cli/commands/ mirroring sprint.ts and register it where existing commands register. Add config under a new history/memory section in src/config/schema.ts with zod defaults. Collocate *.test.ts with Vitest. ESM with .js import specifiers. Unicode box-drawing section headers in long files.

metadata.riskNotes: Only Sprint 1 touches a load-bearing read path (loadHistory); it is guarded by resume-cursor and conformance regression tests that assert byte-equivalence with rotation on and off. Layer 2 is additive, deterministic, LLM-free, and runs outside the autopilot loop, so its blast radius is contained. History is 272 lines today, so Sprint 1 is preventative at-scale hardening rather than an acute fix.
